changes.detail: All five required fields present and correct. work_id: MED-FRAME-1_signal_family_contextual_frame_architecture. branch: work/MED-FRAME-1-signal-family-contextual-frame-architecture. risk_level: STANDARD. execution_model: TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. change_type: CONTENT. Cited: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 2-6.

STANDARD correct. Sprint produces only: (1) docs/architecture/MED-FRAME-1_signal_family_contextual_frame_architecture.md — new documentation file in existing docs/architecture/ directory; (2) optional knowledge_bus/governance/medical_frame_identity_model_draft_v1.yaml — explicitly marked draft/non-runtime. No Intelligence Core files (backend/ssot/, backend/core/pipeline/, backend/core/analytics/, run_work_package.py, golden_gate_local.py, update_cursor_status.py) touched. No runtime behaviour change permitted. STANDARD requires Claude audit only — correct. Cited: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 85-94, 328-337.

CONTENT correct. Sprint is architecture/design only — produces documentation and an optional non-runtime YAML sketch. All code, runtime, frontend, questionnaire schema, drug-category schema, SignalEvaluator, SignalRegistry, and package file changes are explicitly prohibited. No output generation, analytical pipeline, or emitted reasoning can change from this sprint. Cited: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 28-29, 85-94, 328-337.

TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH correct. Docs-Only Bypass (SOP §11) does NOT apply: the optional deliverable knowledge_bus/governance/medical_frame_identity_model_draft_v1.yaml is outside /docs/, so bus is required. Sprint includes baseline checks, STOP conditions, required checks, closure requirements, and success criteria. Cited: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 65-83, 339-404, 340-404.

All 8 required input paths confirmed present in repo. docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_SOP_v1.3.1.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_PASS3_PROMOTION_PROTOCOL_v1.1.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. docs/audit-papers/KB-MAP-1_pass3_to_legacy_package_mapping_and_promotion_plan.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. docs/audit-papers/KB-UTIL-2-PILOT_pass3_to_runtime_artifact_compiler_pilot_report.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. docs/audit-papers/KB-UTIL-2-PROMOTE-WIRE-1_creatinine_runtime_authority_switch_report.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. docs/sprints/launch_core_carry_forward_register.md — CONFIRMED EXISTS. knowledge_bus/governance/pass3_legacy_package_mapping_plan_v1.yaml — CONFIRMED EXISTS. knowledge_bus/governance/pass3_promotion_decision_register_v1.yaml — CONFIRMED EXISTS. Validation scripts: backend/scripts/validate_day_one_architecture.py — CONFIRMED EXISTS. backend/tests/architecture/test_day_one_architecture_guardrails.py — CONFIRMED EXISTS. Output directory: docs/architecture/ — CONFIRMED EXISTS (40+ files present). Optional output dir: knowledge_bus/governance/ — CONFIRMED EXISTS.

Sprint creates docs/architecture/MED-FRAME-1_signal_family_contextual_frame_architecture.md — a new file with no existing equivalent. No other architecture paper covers signal family / contextual frame design. Optional YAML is explicitly marked draft/non-runtime, not a runtime authority. No duplicate source created. Cited: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 269-281.

backend/ssot/questionnaire.json read in full (607 lines). Questionnaire contains 37 fields across 7 sections: demographics, medical_history, symptoms, lifestyle, physical_assessment, cognitive_assessment, family_history. Key modifiers relevant to architecture paper are present: date_of_birth (age derivable), biological_sex, chronic_conditions, long_term_medications, medical_conditions, tobacco_use, alcohol_drinks_weekly, supplements, dietary_pattern, vigorous_exercise_days, resistance_training_days, daily_fluid_intake, sleep_hours_nightly, stress_level_rating, family_cardiovascular_disease, family_diabetes_metabolic. CRITICAL GAP CONFIRMED: long_term_medications field (questionnaire.json lines 139-151) captures only 4 categories: Corticosteroids, Atypical antipsychotics, HIV/AIDS treatments, Statins. NSAIDs, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, diuretics, metformin, thyroid medication, nephrotoxic categories, liver-impacting categories are NOT present in any schema. No standalone drug-category schema file found anywhere in backend/. This gap is the architectural motivation for the sprint and must be accurately reported by Cursor.

KB-UTIL-2-PROMOTE-WIRE-1 report read (docs/audit-papers/KB-UTIL-2-PROMOTE-WIRE-1_creatinine_runtime_authority_switch_report.md). Outcome: activation of pkg_creatinine_high_renal_pass3_v1 was REFUSED. Runtime authority unchanged: pkg_s24_creatinine_high_renal active for signal_creatinine_high::inv_creatinine_high_renal; pkg_kb52c_creatinine_high_reduced_glomerular_filtration active for signal_creatinine_high::inv_creatinine_high_reduced_glomerular_filtration. Promoted candidate not runtime-loaded. This creates exactly the multi-frame architecture context the paper must address: two distinct packages govern two distinct interpretive frames on the same signal, but no formal frame identity model exists. Architecture paper implications section must address this directly.

backend/core/analytics/medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py confirmed present and read (lines 1-60). This assembler produces bounded caveat text from questionnaire long_term_medications and supplements fields. It is NOT a drug-category architecture or a modifier governance model. It handles only 4 medication categories from the questionnaire. Cursor must not treat this assembler as the existing medication-context architecture; it is a narrow output-layer caveat generator, not a Layer B modifier framework. This file must not be modified (out of scope). Cited: backend/core/analytics/medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py lines 11-14.

Baseline STOP conditions appropriate: branch not main, local/remote divergence, dirty working tree, governance docs missing — all checked before work begins. Closure STOP conditions appropriate: branch not matching work/MED-FRAME-1-signal-family-contextual-frame-architecture, non-documentation/governance files changed, ambiguous stash. Validation STOP condition: architecture validators fail. No gap identified — all material failure modes gated. Cited: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 75-83, 390-404.

Out-of-scope section explicitly prohibits: code changes, runtime loading changes, package file changes, SignalEvaluator changes, SignalRegistry changes, frontend changes, questionnaire schema changes, drug-category schema changes, frame evaluation implementation, LLM narrative generation, package activation. STOP conditions enforce at closure. No code path through this sprint's deliverables reaches any Intelligence Core component. Cited: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md lines 328-337.

advisories.title: Questionnaire medication coverage is narrow — architecture paper must report current state accurately
advisories.detail: Current state (read from backend/ssot/questionnaire.json lines 139-151): long_term_medications captures Corticosteroids, Atypical antipsychotics, HIV/AIDS treatments, Statins only. The sprint's context modifier scope list (NSAIDs, ACE inhibitors, ARBs, diuretics, metformin, thyroid medication, nephrotoxics, liver-impacting categories) is TARGET architecture scope, not current schema content. Cursor must report what currently exists, acknowledge the gap, and design the architecture to govern where future categories would live. Do not fabricate schemas that do not exist.

advisories.title: medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py is a caveat text generator, not a drug-category modifier architecture
advisories.detail: Cursor will find backend/core/analytics/medication_caveat_assembler_v1.py when inspecting medication artefacts. This is a narrow Layer C output component that generates bounded caveat sentences from 4 questionnaire-reported medication categories. It must be reported accurately as current state and must not be treated as an existing modifier framework, extended, or modified in this sprint.

advisories.title: Creatinine worked example must reflect KB-UTIL-2-PROMOTE-WIRE-1 outcome — two active packages, no formal frame model
advisories.detail: The creatinine example in the architecture paper should ground itself in the real current state: signal_creatinine_high has two runtime authorities (pkg_s24 covering inv_creatinine_high_renal; pkg_kb52c covering inv_creatinine_high_reduced_glomerular_filtration). The promoted Pass_3 candidate was refused activation due to duplicate key collision. This is exactly the kind of multi-frame identity problem the paper must solve — use it as evidence of why the architecture is needed, not just as a worked hypothetical.

advisories.title: Optional YAML must be marked draft/non-runtime — gate explicitly at closure
advisories.detail: If Cursor creates knowledge_bus/governance/medical_frame_identity_model_draft_v1.yaml, it must be explicitly marked as draft and non-runtime within the file. The closure STOP condition (only documentation/governance draft files changed) covers this, but Cursor should confirm the file contains explicit draft status markers before finishing.
